questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are stated in Articles _____ to _____ of the Constitution.
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are stated in Articles 36 to 51, in Part IV of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Directive Principles are stated in Part _____ of the Indian Constitution.
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are found in Part IV of the Constitution.

questions.question: Directive Principles were inspired by the Constitution of _____.
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles were inspired by the Directive Principles stated in the Constitution of Ireland and principles of Gandhian philosophy.

questions.question: Directive Principles are _____ and cannot be enforced in any court of law.
questions.explanation: Non-justiciable means they cannot be enforced by any court of law.

questions.question: A _____ state is one in which the government plays a key role in protecting and promoting the economic and social well-being of its citizens.
questions.explanation: A welfare state protects and promotes the economic and social well-being of its citizens.

questions.question: The state should ensure equal pay for equal work for both _____ and _____.
questions.explanation: Equal pay for equal work for both men and women is a Directive Principle promoting social and economic equality.

questions.question: The state should impart compulsory and free education to children up to the age of _____ years.
questions.explanation: Free and compulsory education for children up to the age of 14 years is a Directive Principle.

questions.question: The three basic principles of Gandhian thought are Swaraj, Sarvodaya and _____.
questions.explanation: The three basic principles are Swaraj (self-rule), Sarvodaya (welfare for all) and Swavlamban (self-reliance).

questions.question: Sarvodaya means _____ for all.
questions.explanation: Sarvodaya means welfare for all and is one of the basic principles of Gandhian thought.

questions.question: Swavlamban means _____.
questions.explanation: Swavlamban means self-reliance and is one of the three basic principles of Gandhian thought.

questions.question: Village _____ shall be given powers for self-government.
questions.explanation: Under Gandhian Principles, Village Panchayats shall be given powers for self-government.

questions.question: The state shall aim at establishing a uniform _____ code for all citizens.
questions.explanation: Establishing a uniform civil code is a General Directive Principle.

questions.question: The state shall encourage settlement of international disputes by _____.
questions.explanation: Arbitration means mutual agreement, and it is a General Directive Principle for resolving international disputes.

questions.question: Fundamental Rights are _____ and enforceable in the courts.
questions.explanation: Unlike Directive Principles which are non-justiciable, Fundamental Rights are justiciable and enforceable in the courts.

questions.question: Directive Principles and Fundamental Rights are not contradictory but _____.
questions.explanation: Both aim at the same thing and are complementary. Fundamental Rights guarantee political democracy while Directive Principles establish social and economic democracy.

questions.question: The _____ system was abolished and surplus land was distributed among the landless poor.
questions.explanation: The Zamindari system was abolished as an impact of the Directive Principles.

questions.question: _____ has been made a punishable offence as an impact of Directive Principles.
questions.explanation: Untouchability was made a punishable offence as one of the positive impacts of Directive Principles since Independence.

questions.question: The Community Development Programme was launched in _____.
questions.explanation: The Community Development Programme was launched in 1952 to raise the standard of living.

questions.question: To promote cottage industries under Article _____, the government established several boards.
questions.explanation: Under Article 43, the government established boards like Village Industries Board, All India Handicraft Board, Silk Board and Coir Board.

questions.question: The 42nd Amendment added the duty to protect the _____ and safeguard forests and wildlife.
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added a new directive - the duty of the state to protect and improve the environment and to safeguard the forests and wildlife.

questions.question: Under British rule, India was not a _____ state.
questions.explanation: Under British rule, India was not a welfare state as the British were not interested in the welfare of the people.

questions.question: Fundamental Rights guarantee _____ democracy.
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights guarantee political democracy, while Directive Principles establish social and economic democracy.

questions.question: Directive Principles establish social and _____ democracy.
questions.explanation: Directive Principles establish social and economic democracy.

questions.question: The Mahatma Gandhi Rural Employment Guarantee Act was launched in _____.
questions.explanation: The Mahatma Gandhi Rural Employment Guarantee Act was launched in 2006 to raise the standard of living.

questions.question: Gandhi laid stress on rebuilding _____ and promoting cottage industries.
questions.explanation: Gandhian thought laid stress on rebuilding villages and promoting cottage industries.
